rationale: The citing paper uses Stephen and Galak (2012) for a concrete design choice: it explicitly states that channel touchpoints are grouped into paid, owned, and earned categories following that prior work. This categorization is then carried into the empirical setup, descriptive statistics, and model-based channel analyses, so the citation has an operational role beyond a generic literature list mention. At the same time, the citing paper does not build on the cited paper's core earned-media sales model; it primarily borrows the channel framework.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the cited paper's paid-owned-earned media framework to organize and code its multichannel touchpoint data. This is a transfer of a prior conceptual categorization into a new modeling context (path-to-purchase with topic models), rather than a direct extension of the cited paper's main model.
